name: bloom-filter
description: Bloom filter for AQL: a probabilistic set (membership) with no false negatives and a tunable false-positive rate, exposed as the single `Bloom` namespace.

conventions.import: import "./bloom.aql"  — no `end` needed on the pinned build (structure-first engine); the library imports its own aql:* deps, callers need not
conventions.call: receiver-first, verb in the middle, terminated with `end` (or wrapped in parens): `receiver Bloom.verb arg... end`. Without a terminator the verb swallows the following token, giving a wrong result or a dispatch error.
conventions.construct: build filters ONLY via Bloom.make; treat BloomFilter fields as read-only and mutate through the namespace words
conventions.mutation: add and merge MUTATE the receiver in place and return the SAME object — there is no immutable copy
conventions.probabilistic: contains => false is certain (the item was definitely never added); contains => true is probabilistic (false-positive rate ~ p)
conventions.count_vs_added: count is a cardinality ESTIMATE, not a tally; the exact insert count is `added` (read via encode or bf.added)
conventions.import_resolution: relative to the working directory the script is run from, not the importing file
conventions.errors: failures raise coded errors caught with `do [...] error [...]`; read e.code / e.message. Codes: bad_input (Bloom.make args missing or out of range), incompatible_merge (Bloom.merge filters disagree on m or k), bad_payload (Bloom.decode text is not an encode snapshot)
conventions.serialization: encode produces a jsonic snapshot; decode rebuilds an equivalent filter from it (round-trips n, p, m, k, added, and every set bit). Snapshots are portable across processes on the same module version, not across versions that change the hashing.

BloomFilter.note: sealed class; construct only via Bloom.make; fields are read-only. `bits` is an Array of 63-bit integer words.

make.note: n = expected distinct items; p = target false-positive rate in (0, 0.5]. Derives m (bit count) and k (hash count). Raises bad_input on a non-integer/non-positive n or a p outside (0, 0.5].

add.note: MUTATES bf and returns the SAME object. Any value; stringified internally. Sets k bits, increments added.

contains.note: false = definitely never added; true = probably added (false-positive rate ~ p).

count.note: ESTIMATE of distinct items, not a tally. Empty filter => 0. O(m/63).

merge.note: Union of a and b into a. MUTATES a and returns it. Requires identical m and k (same (n, p)); else raises incompatible_merge. O(m/63).

encode.note: jsonic snapshot (params + set-bit indices). Round-trips through Bloom.decode. O(m).

decode.note: Rebuild a filter from a Bloom.encode snapshot; independent of the original. Raises bad_payload on unparseable or incomplete text.
